questions.stem: This two-panel figure contrasts tumor immunity with and without antibodies directed against CTLA-4 and PD-1/PD-L1, showing restored cytotoxic T cell killing when the antibodies are used. What general class of cancer therapy does this illustrate?

explanation.summary: Blocking inhibitory checkpoint pathways (CTLA-4 and PD-1/PD-L1) with antibodies removes the brakes on T cells, restoring their ability to activate and kill tumor cells. This checkpoint blockade approach is approved for advanced solid tumors and some lymphomas.
explanation.whyCorrect: Blocking inhibitory checkpoint pathways (CTLA-4 and PD-1/PD-L1) with antibodies removes the brakes on T cells, restoring their ability to activate and kill tumor cells. This checkpoint blockade approach is approved for advanced solid tumors and some lymphomas.

questions.stem: The tumor cell on the left has lost a specific surface molecule, and as a result the T cell on the right is unable to recognize it. The absence of this molecule most directly impairs which immune function?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Recognition and killing of the tumor cell by cytotoxic CD8+ T cells

choices.label: C
choices.text: Helper CD4+ T cell cytokine release

choices.label: E
choices.text: Activation of the classical complement pathway

explanation.summary: The tumor is MHC class I-deficient. Because cytotoxic T cells identify targets via antigen presented on MHC class I, loss of this molecule allows the tumor to escape CD8+ T cell-mediated killing.
explanation.whyCorrect: The tumor is MHC class I-deficient. Because cytotoxic T cells identify targets via antigen presented on MHC class I, loss of this molecule allows the tumor to escape CD8+ T cell-mediated killing.
